items.title: SwitchTemplate
items.text: Template switching plugin for MODx Revolution. It changes the template of a MODX resource on the fly with a request parameter.

items.title: Requirements
items.text:
• MODX Revolution 2.6+
  • PHP 7.2+

items.title: Features
items.text:
• Custom Manager Page for the settings.
  • Cache the output of the page with the switched template.
  • Switch the template of a MODX resource on base of the resource extension.
  • Filter the output of a MODX resource to different markup.

items.title: License
items.text: The project is licensed under the GPLv2 license.

items.title: Changelog
items.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

items.title: Removed
items.text: • AMP Output filtering (because of Guzzle 6 vs. 7 issues in MODX 3 and since AMP is dead)

items.title: Security
items.text: • Update guzzlehttp/guzzle to 6.5.8 for security reasons

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• Update Composer dependencies
  • Decrease the timeout in the statistics resolver
  • Fix wrong classname

items.title: Security
items.text: • Update guzzlehttp/guzzle to 6.5.7 for security reasons

items.title: Fixed
items.text:
• Fix strrpos warning: Offset is greater than the length of haystack string
  • Fix PHP notice: Undefined property: SwitchTemplate::$fromCache

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• Code refactoring
  • Full MODX 3 compatibility

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Bugfix for not found switching extensions

items.title: Added
items.text: • Add static file support for templates (Thanks to David Pede) [#10]

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Show debug mode in custom manager page
  • System settings tab in custom manager page

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Log errors in debug mode only

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Bugfix for an empty output type

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Make the extension based template switch work with xRouting, LangRouter and other routing extras
  • Enable debug information in the frontend with a system setting and an URL parameter

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Switch the template on base of the extension
  • $modx->RegClient methods are executed in switched templates with HTML output
  • Filter the page source before output i.e. for AMP output
  • Absolute links & images and image width & height attributes in AMP output
  • Switched template/chunk name on base of the original template name
  • Set the allowed SwitchTemplate modes in a template variable on resource base

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Switch back to default template if the chunk/template is not found
  • Template name could be set by placeholder too

items.title: Added
items.text: • Chunk name for chunk template type could be set by placeholder

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Processed placeholders are now cached

items.title: Added
items.text: • Initial public release

items.title: Contributors
items.text:
The SwitchTemplate project was started in 2014 by Thomas Jakobi.

Many thanks to all who contributed, whether by creating pull requests, submitting bug reports, or donating.

items.title: Install from MODX Extras
items.text: Search for SwitchTemplate in the Package Manager of a MODX installation and install it in there.

items.title: Manual installation
items.text:
If you can't access the MODX Extras Repository in your MODX installation, you can manually install SwitchTemplate.

  • Download the transport package from MODX Extras (or one of the pre built transport packages in _packages)
  • Upload the zip file to your MODX installation's core/packages folder or upload it manually in the MODX Package Manager.
  • In the MODX Manager, navigate to the Package Manager page, and select 'Search locally for packages' from the dropdown button.
  • SwitchTemplate should now show up in the list of available packages. Click the corresponding 'Install' button and follow the instructions to complete the installation.

items.title: Build it from source
items.text:
To build and install the package from source you could use Git Package Management. The GitHub repository of SwitchTemplate contains a config.json to build that package locally. Use this option, if you want to debug SwitchTemplate and/or contribute bugfixes and enhancements.

After downloading the github repository, you have to change into the core/components/switchtemplate folder and install mpdf with composer by invoking composer install.

items.title: How it works
items.text:
SwitchTemplate template switching plugin for MODx Revolution. It changes the template of a MODX resource on the fly with a request parameter. Different settings with different setting keys could be set in a custom manager page.

With each setting a different template could be used to output the resource. As well the template type could be set to chunk or template. If the template type is chunk the resource variables and template variables are prepared as placeholder before the chunk is parsed. The caching of the output of SwitchTemplate could be enabled for each separate setting. The output is cached separate for each resource and each setting. Each setting could be enabled or disabled for selected resources.

SwitchTemplate could be used for different purposes: As Ajax connector, as language switcher ...

items.title: System Settings
items.text:
SwitchTemplate uses the following system settings in the namespace switchtemplate:

Setting Description Default allow_debug_info Enable this setting to show switchtemplate debug information for a page if it is called with the GET parameter &switchtemplate-debug=1. CAUTION: Disable the setting after you have debugged your installation. The debug information exposes a lot information of your site! No cache_resource_expires The cache expiration time in seconds for custom template output. 0 means indefinitely or until the cache items are purposely cleared. 0 cache_resource_handler The class of cache handler for SwitchTemplate to use. xPDOFileCache cache_resource_key The key identifying a cache handler for SwitchTemplate to use. resource debug Log debug information in the MODX error log. No mode_key Request key used to switch the custom template chunks. mode mode_tv Name of a template variable that contains a comma-separated list of allowed switch modes. -

items.title: Custom Manager Page
items.text:
SwitchTemplate could be configured by settings created in a custom manager page. Each setting could use the following properties:

Property Description Name A name to identify this configuration. Key The request key value that selects this setting. Extension A file extension (including the dot), that selects this setting. Chunk/Template Name The name of a chunk or a template that is parsed and displayed if this setting is used. The name of the chunk is variable by using MODX placeholders. The placeholders are filled with the values of the current resource (no template variables). If this chunk is not found, a chunkname with stripped placeholders is used. If this field is empty, the name of the original template is used with the setting key as suffix (separated with a space). In this case the field value in the grid is green. Template Type The template type that is parsed and displayed (Chunk or Template). Cache the Output Cache the output of the switched template. Enabled Resources (Default 'All') A list of MODX resources that could have a switched template. Disabled Resources (Default 'None') A list of MODX resources that could not have a switched template.

items.title: Example
items.text:
If you create the following setting in the custom manager page, you could call every page in your installation with the url parameter mode=ajax (i.e. https://your.own.domain/?mode=ajax) or with the file extension .ajax.html (i.e. https://your.own.domain/index.ajax.html) and the page is shown with the template chunk ajaxTest.

Property Value Name Test Key ajax Extension .ajax.html Chunk/Template Name ajaxTest Template Type Chunk Cache the Output Yes Enabled Resources (Default 'All') - Disabled Resources (Default 'None') -
